subject: [PATCH v4 0/4] arm64: cross-CPU NMI via SDEI

reviews.author: Doug Anderson
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Doug Anderson noted that the patch does not handle the case where SDEI is disabled, which would prevent the cross-CPU NMI mechanism from working.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
Hi,

On Wed, Jun 17, 2026 at 12:20 PM Kiryl Shutsemau <kirill@shutemov.name> wrote:

reviews.author: Doug Anderson
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Doug Anderson pointed out that the patch does not provide a clear explanation of how the SDEI event is delivered to the target CPU, and requested more information on this critical aspect of the mechanism.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
Hi,

On Wed, Jun 17, 2026 at 12:20 PM Kiryl Shutsemau <kirill@shutemov.name> wrote:

2026-06-17.patch_summary: This patch series adds a new mechanism to deliver NMI (Non-Maskable Interrupt) across CPUs on arm64 architecture using SDEI (System Description Event Infrastructure). This is necessary for certain debug and observability features, such as capturing CPU backtraces and crash dumps, which require interrupting a CPU that has its interrupts locally masked. The existing pseudo-NMI mechanism is too expensive in terms of performance overhead, so this new approach uses SDEI to deliver the NMI at a lower cost. This allows for more accurate debugging and observability features without introducing significant performance regressions.

reviews.author: Julian Braha
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Julian Braha noted that the patch does not include a Reviewed-by tag from the SDEI firmware maintainers, which is required for this feature to be considered stable and production-ready.

The reviewer pointed out that the dependency on ARM64 is redundant and can be replaced by the existing dependency on ARM_SDE_INTERFACE, suggesting a comment to clarify this instead of removing the explicit dependency.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Kiryl,

On 6/17/26 20:20, Kiryl Shutsemau wrote:

---

The dependency on ARM64 is redundant here since you already have the
dependency on ARM_SDE_INTERFACE. Maybe a comment instead, though I think
it's pretty clear from the prompt...


- Julian Braha

2026-06-18.patch_summary: This patch series adds a new mechanism to deliver NMI (Non-Maskable Interrupt) across CPUs on arm64 architecture using SDEI (System Description Event Infrastructure). This is necessary for certain debug and observability features, such as capturing CPU backtraces and crash dumps, which require interrupting a CPU that has its interrupts locally masked. The existing pseudo-NMI mechanism is too expensive in terms of performance overhead, so this new approach uses SDEI to deliver the NMI at a lower cost. This allows for more accurate debugging and observability features without introducing significant performance regressions.

reviews.author: Catalin Marinas
reviews.summary:
The reviewer pointed out that the patch does not handle the case where SDEI is disabled in firmware, which would prevent the cross-CPU NMI mechanism from working.

The reviewer asked whether the firmware sets ICC_CTLR_EL1.PMHE and requested numbers comparing the performance of the patch with and without a data synchronization barrier (DSB) on the enable path.

The reviewer expressed concern that adding new functionality to SDEI, which is being deprecated, would be counterproductive and suggested not pursuing it further.
sentiment_signals: handling of SDEI disabled in firmware
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Kiryl,

On Wed, Jun 17, 2026 at 08:20:01PM +0100, Kiryl Shutsemau wrote:

---

Does your firmware set ICC_CTLR_EL1.PMHE? I'd be curious to see the
numbers if the DSB was omitted on the enable path.

---

The direction of travel is to deprecated SDEI. I wouldn't add more stuff
on top of this interface.

(I haven't looked at the patches yet; Marc/Mark/James are more
knowledgeable than me in this area)

-- 
Catalin

reviews.author: Marc Zyngier
reviews.summary:
The reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where the target CPU is already in an SDEI event handler, which could lead to a deadlock.

Reviewer Marc Zyngier questioned the claim of significant overhead on pseudo-NMI and requested actual profiling data to understand the issue.
reviews.raw_body:
On Fri, 19 Jun 2026 15:00:26 +0100,
Catalin Marinas <catalin.marinas@arm.com> wrote:

---

I certainly don't observe this sort of overhead on the HW I have
access to, and would like to understand where this is coming from with
actual profiling data.

	M.

-- 
Without deviation from the norm, progress is not possible.

2026-06-19.patch_summary: This patch series adds a new mechanism to deliver NMI (Non-Maskable Interrupt) across CPUs on arm64 architecture using SDEI (System Description Event Infrastructure). This is necessary for certain debug and observability features, such as capturing CPU backtraces and crash dumps, which require interrupting a CPU that has its interrupts locally masked. The existing pseudo-NMI mechanism is too expensive in terms of performance overhead, so this new approach uses SDEI to deliver the NMI at a lower cost. This allows for more accurate debugging and observability features without introducing significant performance regressions.
